project.name: LLM Gateway
project.description: LLM API security proxy gateway — intercepts, scans, and audits all OpenAI-compatible API requests

sprints.name: Phase 1 - Core Proxy MVP
sprints.goal: Build a working OpenAI-compatible proxy that forwards chat completions (non-streaming + streaming SSE), embeddings, and models requests to upstream providers, driven by config-based routing

features.title: Project scaffold & Fastify entrypoint
features.description: Initialize TypeScript project with Fastify 5, configure tsconfig (strict mode), vitest, pnpm, and create the entrypoint src/index.ts with a health-check route. Set up project directory structure per DESIGN.md.
acceptance_criteria: Given the project is cloned, when `pnpm install && pnpm dev` is run, then Fastify starts and responds to GET /health with 200
TypeScript strict mode compiles without errors
Vitest test runner executes (even with 0 tests)
Directory structure matches DESIGN.md layout
features.technical_notes: Use pnpm 10, tsx for dev runtime, tsc for build. Fastify register pattern for route organization.

features.title: Configuration system (zod + TOML)
features.description: Implement config parsing with zod + smol-toml: read config.toml, validate provider definitions (name, baseUrl, apiKey, model aliases). Export typed config object for use across the app.
acceptance_criteria: Given a valid config.toml with providers, when the app starts, then config is parsed into a typed object
Given an invalid config (missing required field), when the app starts, then it exits with a clear zod error message
Provider entries contain: name, baseUrl, apiKey, modelMappings (alias -> real model name)
features.technical_notes: Use smol-toml for TOML parsing. Define zod schema for PORT, LOG_LEVEL, PROVIDERS. Provider config is the foundation for routing.

features.title: Model-to-provider router
features.description: Implement the router that resolves a requested model name to an upstream provider (baseUrl + apiKey). Support model alias mapping from config and pass-through for unknown models to a default provider.
acceptance_criteria: Given a request for model 'gpt-4o' which maps to provider 'openai', when routing, then the correct baseUrl and apiKey are returned
Given a request for an aliased model 'fast-chat' mapped to 'claude-3-haiku', when routing, then the resolved model name is 'claude-3-haiku' on the mapped provider
Given no matching provider and no default, when routing, then return a 404-style error
features.technical_notes: Pure function, no I/O. Config-driven: router reads from the parsed provider config.

features.title: Non-streaming chat completions proxy
features.description: Implement POST /v1/chat/completions that forwards the request body to the upstream provider determined by the router. Handle request/response transformation, error forwarding, and correct OpenAI-compatible response headers.
acceptance_criteria: Given a valid chat completions request with stream:false, when proxied, then the upstream response is returned to the client with identical JSON structure
Given the upstream returns an error (4xx/5xx), when proxied, then the error is forwarded with correct status code and OpenAI error format
Given a request with a model alias, when proxied, then the model name is resolved before forwarding
features.technical_notes: Use native fetch for upstream calls. Keep forwarder generic for reuse across endpoints.

features.title: Streaming SSE chat completions proxy
features.description: Extend the chat completions proxy to support stream:true. Implement SSE parsing with eventsource-parser via Web Streams TransformStream, handling partial line buffering across TCP packets and the data: [DONE] special case.
acceptance_criteria: Given a chat completions request with stream:true, when proxied, then SSE chunks are relayed to the client in real-time with correct content-type: text/event-stream
Given partial TCP packets, when the SSE parser buffers, then lines are correctly reassembled before processing
Given 'data: [DONE]' from upstream, when parsed, then it is forwarded as-is and the stream closes cleanly
features.technical_notes: Core technical challenge per DESIGN.md. TransformStream design should anticipate PII scanning hooks (Phase 3).

features.title: /v1/models passthrough
features.description: Implement GET /v1/models that forwards to the upstream provider. Aggregate models from all configured providers.
acceptance_criteria: Given a GET /v1/models request, when proxied, then a list of available models is returned in OpenAI format
Given multiple providers configured, when models are listed, then models from all providers are included
features.technical_notes: Aggregate from all providers. Response matches OpenAI /v1/models schema.

features.title: /v1/embeddings passthrough
features.description: Implement POST /v1/embeddings that forwards embedding requests to the upstream provider.
acceptance_criteria: Given a valid embeddings request, when proxied, then the upstream response is returned with correct embedding vectors
Given an upstream error, when proxied, then the error is forwarded with correct status

features.title: End-to-end integration tests
features.description: Write vitest integration tests that spin up the gateway and a mock upstream server. Verify the full request lifecycle: non-streaming chat, streaming SSE chat, models listing, and embeddings.
acceptance_criteria: Integration test suite starts a real Fastify instance and a mock upstream
Non-streaming and streaming chat completions are proxied correctly
Error responses from upstream are forwarded with correct status codes
All tests pass with `pnpm test`
features.technical_notes: Use vitest with a mock HTTP server. Test the full Fastify lifecycle.

sprints.name: Phase 2 - Auth & Rate Limiting
sprints.goal: Add virtual API Key authentication with argon2 hashing, SQLite persistence, in-memory sliding window rate limiting (RPM/TPM), and admin CRUD endpoints for key management

features.title: SQLite database initialization + migration
features.description: Initialize better-sqlite3 as the database layer. Create the 001-init.sql migration with tables for virtual keys (id, name, key_hash, rate_limits, created_at, revoked_at), upstream providers (id, name, encrypted_api_key, base_url, created_at), and audit_logs (id, request_id, timestamp, api_key_id, model, endpoint, prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, cost_usd, latency_ms, status, pii_detected, pii_types_found, prompt_injection_score, content_hash_sha256). Implement db connection lifecycle managed by Fastify plugins.
acceptance_criteria: Given the app starts, when db initializes, then all tables from 001-init.sql are created in the configured SQLite file path
Given the db is already initialized, when the app restarts, then migrations are not re-run (idempotent)
Given an invalid migration SQL, when the app starts, then it exits with a clear error message
Unit tests verify table creation and basic CRUD operations work
features.technical_notes: Use better-sqlite3 synchronous API (single-threaded, fits Node.js event loop). Register as a Fastify plugin with onClose hook to close the db connection. Migration table tracks applied migrations. Config for db path (default: ./data/gateway.db). Need to add better-sqlite3 and @types/better-sqlite3 to dependencies.

features.title: Crypto utilities (AES-256-GCM + argon2)
features.description: Implement encryption utilities for two purposes: (1) AES-256-GCM encryption/decryption for upstream provider API keys stored in DB, using a master encryption key from config; (2) Argon2id hashing for virtual API keys (client-facing) with proper salt generation. Also implement constant-time key comparison to prevent timing attacks.
acceptance_criteria: Given a plaintext API key and a master key, when encrypt() is called, then the result is a deterministic-format string containing IV + authTag + ciphertext
Given an encrypted API key, when decrypt() is called with the same master key, then the original plaintext is recovered
Given a plaintext virtual key, when hash() is called, then an argon2id hash is produced with a random salt
Given a plaintext key and a stored hash, when verify() is called, then it returns true if they match and false otherwise, using constant-time comparison
All crypto operations have unit tests including edge cases (empty strings, very long keys)
features.technical_notes: Use Node.js built-in crypto for AES-256-GCM. Use argon2 npm package for hashing (native bindings, faster than pure JS). Master encryption key comes from config.toml [security] section. Export encrypt/decrypt/hash/verify functions. Need to add argon2 to dependencies.

features.title: Virtual API Key storage layer
features.description: Implement the data access layer for virtual API keys in SQLite. CRUD operations: createKey (generate random key, hash with argon2, store hash + metadata), getKeyById, listKeys (pagination), revokeKey (soft delete with revoked_at timestamp), updateRateLimits. Include upstream provider key storage with AES-256-GCM encryption. Add an in-memory LRU cache for key lookups by ID to avoid hitting SQLite on every request.
acceptance_criteria: Given a new key request with name and rate limits, when createKey() is called, then a virtual key (gwk_...) is generated, its argon2 hash is stored in DB, and the plaintext is returned once
Given a key ID, when getKeyById() is called, then key metadata (id, name, rate_limits, created_at, revoked) is returned from cache or DB
Given a revoked key ID, when getKeyById() is called, then the key is returned with revoked=true and revoked_at timestamp
Given multiple keys exist, when listKeys() is called with offset/limit, then paginated results are returned
Given an upstream provider with plaintext API key, when stored, then the key is encrypted with AES-256-GCM before writing to DB
Unit tests cover all CRUD operations with an in-memory SQLite database
features.technical_notes: Use better-sqlite3 prepared statements for performance. Key format: 'gwk_' + 32 random hex chars. LRU cache with TTL (5 min default) for hot key lookups. Cache invalidation on revoke/update. In-memory SQLite (:memory:) for tests.

features.title: Request authentication middleware
features.description: Implement Fastify authentication middleware that: extracts Bearer token from Authorization header, verifies the virtual key against stored hashes (argon2), attaches key metadata (id, name, rate limits) to the request context, and returns proper OpenAI-compatible error responses (401 for invalid/expired keys, missing header). Integrate with the key LRU cache for fast lookups on subsequent requests.
acceptance_criteria: Given a valid Bearer token in Authorization header, when the auth middleware runs, then request.apiKey is populated with { id, name, rateLimits }

Given an invalid or expired Bearer token, when the auth middleware runs, then a 401 response is returned with OpenAI error format: { error: { message, type: 'invalid_request_error', code: 'invalid_api_key' } }
Given a missing Authorization header, when the auth middleware runs, then a 401 response is returned
Given a revoked key, when the auth middleware runs, then a 401 response is returned with message indicating the key has been revoked
Given the same valid key on repeated requests, when the cache is warm, then argon2 verification is skipped and cached metadata is used
Auth middleware applies to /v1/* routes but NOT to /admin/* routes (admin has its own auth via config)
Unit tests with mock key store verify all auth scenarios
features.technical_notes: Use Fastify onRequest hook. Cache stores { id, name, rateLimits } after successful verification. Cache entry invalidated on revoke. argon2 verify is intentionally slow (~100ms) — cache is critical for performance. Admin routes use a separate static admin token from config.

features.title: In-memory sliding window rate limiter
features.description: Implement a sliding window rate limiter supporting RPM (requests per minute), TPM (tokens per minute), and RPD (requests per day) limits per virtual key. Use an in-memory data structure (Map of sorted arrays or circular buffers) with automatic cleanup of expired windows. Configurable default limits and per-key overrides.
acceptance_criteria: Given a key with RPM=60, when 60 requests arrive within 1 minute, then the 61st request returns a rate limit check result of { allowed: false, retryAfterMs: <remaining ms> }
Given a key with TPM=100000, when 100000 tokens are consumed within 1 minute, then the next request exceeding the limit is rejected
Given a key with RPD=1000, when 1000 requests arrive within 24 hours, then the 1001st is rejected
Given requests from 2 minutes ago, when the sliding window check runs, then those old requests are excluded from the count
Given a key with no per-key limits, when the check runs, then global default limits from config are applied
Given rate limit rejection, when the response is formed, then retryAfterMs is a positive integer indicating when to retry
Memory usage grows bounded — old entries are cleaned up automatically
Unit tests verify all window types and edge cases (burst requests, exactly-at-limit)
features.technical_notes: Use a Map<string, RateLimitState> where each entry stores timestamped request counts. Sliding window: count requests in [now - windowSize, now]. Cleanup: periodically prune entries older than the largest window (24h). No Redis needed — single-node in-memory. Export checkRateLimit(keyId, tokenCount) function.

features.title: Rate limit middleware integration
features.description: Integrate the rate limiter into the Fastify request pipeline as a middleware that runs after auth but before proxy forwarding. On rate limit exceeded, return HTTP 429 with OpenAI-compatible error format and standard rate limit headers (X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset). Also return these headers on successful requests.
acceptance_criteria: Given a request that passes auth, when the rate limit check passes, then the request proceeds to proxy forwarding and response includes X-RateLimit-* headers
Given a request that passes auth, when RPM is exceeded, then a 429 response is returned with { error: { message, type: 'rate_limit_error', code: 'rate_limit_exceeded' } } and Retry-After header
Given a streaming request, when TPM is tracked, then token count from the response is counted toward the rate limit (best-effort: count from response usage field)
Given a successful non-streaming response, when it includes usage.prompt_tokens + usage.completion_tokens, then those tokens are counted toward TPM
Rate limit headers appear on all successful /v1/* responses
Integration test with mocked upstream verifies the full auth -> rate limit -> proxy pipeline
features.technical_notes: Use Fastify preHandler hook, runs after auth onRequest hook. Token counting for TPM: non-streaming reads from response.usage; streaming estimates from input tokens only (output unknown until complete). Add rate limit state to request context for downstream use.

features.title: Admin API - Virtual Key CRUD
features.description: Implement admin endpoints for virtual API key management. Endpoints: POST /admin/keys (create key with name + optional rate limits), GET /admin/keys (list all keys with pagination), GET /admin/keys/:id (get key details), DELETE /admin/keys/:id (revoke key), PATCH /admin/keys/:id (update key name/rate limits). Admin endpoints are protected by a static admin token from config (separate from virtual keys).
acceptance_criteria: Given a valid admin token, when POST /admin/keys with { name: 'test', rateLimits: { rpm: 60, tpm: 100000, rpd: 1000 } }, then a new key is created and { id, name, key: 'gwk_...', createdAt } is returned (key shown only once)
Given a valid admin token, when GET /admin/keys is called, then a paginated list of key metadata (without hashes) is returned
Given a valid admin token, when GET /admin/keys/:id is called, then key details are returned
Given a valid admin token, when DELETE /admin/keys/:id is called, then the key is soft-deleted (revoked_at set) and 200 is returned
Given a valid admin token, when PATCH /admin/keys/:id with { rateLimits: { rpm: 120 } }, then rate limits are updated
Given an invalid or missing admin token, when any /admin/* endpoint is called, then 401 is returned
Given a request to create a key without a name, when POST /admin/keys is called, then 400 is returned with validation error
All endpoints have integration tests using an in-memory SQLite database
features.technical_notes: Use a separate admin auth middleware checking against config [admin] token. Fastify route registration under /admin prefix. Zod schema for request body validation. The plaintext key is returned ONLY on POST create — never stored, never retrievable again. Use Fastify schema validation for request/response.

features.title: Phase 2 integration tests
features.description: Write end-to-end integration tests covering the full Phase 2 pipeline: key creation via admin API, authenticated requests with valid/invalid/revoked keys, rate limit enforcement (RPM/TPM), 429 responses with correct headers, and the complete request lifecycle from auth through rate limit through proxy forwarding.
acceptance_criteria: Given a created virtual key, when a /v1/chat/completions request is made with that key, then the request is proxied successfully
Given a valid key that has exceeded RPM, when a request is made, then 429 is returned with Retry-After header
Given a revoked key, when a request is made, then 401 is returned
Given an admin token, when admin CRUD operations are performed, then they work correctly
Given rate limit headers on a successful response, when checked, then X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset are present
All Phase 2 integration tests pass alongside Phase 1 tests with `pnpm test`
features.technical_notes: Extend the existing test helpers (mock-upstream.ts) to support auth headers. Use an in-memory SQLite database for test isolation. Test the full Fastify lifecycle including middleware chain. Verify that Phase 1 routes still work without auth (backward compatibility) if auth is not configured.
